description_long: This is a round-bodied ceramic vase with a single, thick handle connecting the neck to the shoulder. The neck is short and flares outward into a wide, slightly irregular rim with a small central opening. The surface is decorated with faded, dotted and linear patterns in dark brown and black, radiating from the neck down the body, interspersed with lighter, weathered patches. The handle is robust and features a series of dark dots along its length, adding to the decorative motif. The overall finish is matte and rough, with visible signs of age and surface wear. The vase appears to be hand-crafted, likely intended for decorative or utilitarian use in an urban or historical setting.
description: A weathered, round ceramic vase with a wide rim, single handle, and faded dotted and linear patterns in earthy tones.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide rim, central opening, and the handle attached to the right side, with visible dotted and linear patterns.
description_view_1: Rear view dominated by the handle, with dotted decoration running along its length and more linear patterns visible on the body.
description_view_2: Side view highlighting the handle's attachment and the weathered, patchy surface with both dark and light areas.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the full roundness of the body, faded patterns, and the absence of the handle in this perspective.
